name: New Year's Day, Vasilopitta Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp Each.. cinnamon, anise seeds, orange peel
recipeInstructions:
The Greeks wrap coins in foil and knead them into this bread. They believe anyone who finds a coin will have good luck in the coming year.
Dissolve yeast in warm water and set aside. Put spices, orange peel, bay leaf, and ouzo in a pot with 1/2 cup water and bring to a boil. Set aside to steep and cool. Heat milk and remove from heat. Add butter and allow the milk to cool slightly. Put flour, sugar, and salt in a large bowl. Mix eggs, yeast mixture, and flavored water (remove only the bay leaf). Stir until all the flour is absorbed.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured board and knead until smooth, about 20 minutes. Place in a buttered bowl, flipping to grease top as well. Cover with a towel dampened with hot water. Set in a warm, draft-free place to rise until doubled in bulk, about 4-6 hours.
Punch down dough and knead for about 5 minutes. Shape into one large round loaf and decorate the top with walnut halves. Set aside to rise once more, about 2 hours (uncovered). Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 1 hour.
Makes 1 loaf.
